extension_description.message: Manage server-side Sieve mail filters in mailcow directly from Thunderbird/Betterbird.

rule_stop_after_help.message: When this rule matches, no further rules are applied to the message. Without this option, every rule whose conditions match will run — for example, the same message can both move to a folder and be flagged.

warn_redirect.message: Redirects are visible to the sender.

warn_discard.message: The message will be deleted without saving and cannot be recovered.

err_no_network.message: No connection to the mail server. Check your internet connection.

err_auth.message: Server access denied. Contact your administrator.

err_auth_password_wrong.message: The password is incorrect. Open settings and update it.

err_server.message: The mail server returned an error. Please try again later.

err_no_managed_config.message: No configuration found. Open settings and enter the connection details.

options_source_managed.message: Source: Enterprise Policy (URL set by policy).

options_source_none.message: No configuration. Enter the connection details, or ask your administrator to provide a policies.json (see the README for an example).

options_mailbox_info.message: All rules will be applied to mailbox $NAME$.

options_mailbox_unknown.message: Mailbox is not set. Specify it in the field above.

mgr_no_config_text.message: Open the connection settings and enter the details (middleware URL and password).

mgr_no_config_partial.message: The middleware URL is set by your administrator. Enter the password in the settings.

mgr_confirm_delete.message: Delete rule “$NAME$”? This cannot be undone.

ed_apply_when_info.message: Sieve filters run on the server when a message is delivered. Local Thunderbird triggers do not apply.

ed_confirm_discard.message: Unsaved changes will be lost. Close the editor?

wiz_template_sender_desc.message: The most common template: the sender address is used as the From criterion. All correspondence with this contact is moved to the chosen folder.

wiz_template_domain_desc.message: Match by the sender's domain. Useful when you want to gather all mail from a company or service into one folder.

wiz_template_recipient_desc.message: Match by the To address. Handy when you receive mail at several aliases and want to sort by which one.

wiz_template_reply_to_desc.message: Match by the Reply-To header. Useful for mailing lists that set an explicit reply address.

wiz_template_no_reply_to.message: The message has no Reply-To header.

wiz_template_subject_desc.message: The filter triggers when the subject contains the given fragment. The filter name is taken from the subject (truncated to 40 characters).

wiz_template_list_desc.message: Match by the List-Id header. Ideal for mailing-list traffic.

wiz_template_no_list_id.message: The message has no List-Id header.

wiz_template_tags_desc.message: This template uses Thunderbird tags. Coming in a future release.

wiz_template_tags_disabled.message: The tags template will be available in a future release.

wiz_template_recipient_disabled_own.message: The message is addressed only to your own addresses — “By recipient” would not be useful here.

wiz_msg_error.message: Could not load the message details.

wiz_no_message.message: Open a message and try again.

ctx_create_filter_from_message.message: Create a filter from this message…

mgr_lazy_auth_info.message: The plugin could not get the password for mailbox {0} from the Thunderbird settings.

mgr_lazy_auth_step_open.message: Open Thunderbird Account Settings (menu → Edit → Account Settings).

mgr_lazy_auth_step_save.message: In Server Settings of this mailbox, enter the password and tick “Remember password”.

mgr_lazy_auth_step_master.message: If you set a Thunderbird primary (master) password, unlock it — the prompt will appear on the next mail check.

mgr_lazy_auth_still_missing.message: The password is still not available. Make sure it is saved in Account Settings and the primary password is unlocked.

mgr_no_imap_accounts.message: Thunderbird has no configured IMAP/POP3 accounts. Add a mail account in Thunderbird first.

err_no_password.message: Save the mailbox password in Thunderbird Account Settings — the plugin will read it from there.

options_no_accounts.message: No IMAP/POP3 accounts found in Thunderbird.

options_import_unavailable.message: Reading the password from Thunderbird settings is unavailable — this Thunderbird version does not support the plugin's Experiment API.

options_migration_failed.message: Legacy configuration for {0} was not migrated automatically (account not found in Thunderbird). Please enter the password manually.

options_notify_help.message: When a rule moves an incoming message into a sub-folder (for example, INBOX/Newsletters), Thunderbird by default refreshes the unread counter for that sub-folder only when you open it. To keep the counter up to date for sub-folders automatically, enable the check for the accounts you care about. The lightweight IMAP STATUS command is used, which puts almost no load on the server.

options_notify_hint.message: Changes take effect on the next mail check.

options_notify_server_overrides.message: The account-wide toggle above is on — Thunderbird polls all sub-folders. Individual checkboxes have no effect right now.

options_notify_unsubscribed_hint.message: Folder is not subscribed — Thunderbird won't poll it. Subscribe via Edit → Subscribe.

options_beh_strip_prefixes.message: Strip subject prefixes when creating a “By subject” filter

options_beh_hide_system_folders.message: Hide system folders (Trash, Junk, Drafts, …) in folder pickers

options_beh_exclude_own.message: Exclude my own email addresses when creating a “By recipient” filter

options_beh_new_position_end.message: At the end of the list (default)

options_beh_new_position_top.message: At the top of the list

options_pwd_managed_by_tb.message: The password is read directly from Thunderbird settings (Account Settings → Server Settings) every time. The plugin never stores it.

options_pwd_tb_unavailable_long.message: Open Thunderbird Account Settings → Server Settings, enter the password and tick “Remember password”. If you set a Thunderbird primary (master) password, unlock it.

options_pwd_experiment_unavailable.message: This Thunderbird version does not support reading the password from settings (no Experiment API). Update Thunderbird or use a build with Experiment API support.

options_url_auto_long.message: Middleware URL was derived automatically from the IMAP host: $URL$

options_url_managed_long.message: Middleware URL is set by an Enterprise Policy: $URL$

options_url_none_long.message: Middleware URL is not set. Configure it under “Advanced”.

mgr_run_in_folder.message: Run the selected rule on folder:

run_no_messages.message: The folder has no messages to process.

run_skipped_redirect.message: The “redirect” action was skipped: it only runs at delivery time on the server.

run_no_selection.message: Select a rule from the list first.

import_no_experiment.message: Import is unavailable in this Thunderbird version (Experiment API missing).

import_empty.message: No local filters were found for this account.

import_summary.message: Will import $N$ of $T$. The rest will be skipped.

import_cleanup_label.message: Delete local Thunderbird filters after import

import_cleanup_hint.message: After successful import, delete the selected filters from Thunderbird's settings. This cannot be undone.

import_cleanup_help.message: After successful import, delete the selected filters from Thunderbird's settings. This cannot be undone.

import_cleanup_confirm.message: Delete $N$ local filter(s) from Thunderbird? This cannot be undone.

options_import_local_unavailable.message: Import is unavailable in this Thunderbird version.

options_import_local_empty.message: No local filters were found for this account.

options_import_local_no_compat.message: Found $N$ filter(s); none are compatible — import is not possible.

mgr_confirm_delete_many.message: Delete the selected rules ($N$ in total)?
